birth-tail.description: The character possesses a functional tail fully integrated into their anatomy, allowing improved balance, spatial awareness, and natural rear coverage in physical situations.
birth-tail.effet: The tail may be used as an additional limb, limited by a dedicated skill acting as a cap (similar to ambidexterity). It cannot wield tools or manufactured weapons, restricting combat actions to natural weapons only. The tail counts as a natural weapon based on **AGI**, and the character suffers no penalties when attacked from behind or when flanked.

birth-horns.description: The character bears prominent horns that extend their body's natural lethality and reinforce their presence in close combat encounters.
birth-horns.effet: The horns count as natural weapons usable without equipment. Horn-based attacks are treated as natural weapon attacks (attribute is typically **STR**) and may be used for actions such as charges or headbutts.

birth-winged-creature.description: The character possesses fully developed wings adapted for sustained aerial movement and precise control while flying.
birth-winged-creature.effet: The character gains the ability to fly, benefits from good maneuverability while airborne (180°), and unlocks the **Flight** skill.

birth-amphibious.description: The character has biological adaptations that allow them to function efficiently in aquatic environments, including prolonged underwater activity.
birth-amphibious.effet: The character gains good maneuverability while swimming (180°), can hold their breath far longer than a standard individual, and gains advantage on Swimming-related tests.

birth-quadruped.description: The character moves using four limbs dedicated to locomotion, providing exceptional stability, speed, and resistance to displacement.
birth-quadruped.effet: The character gains **+5 Defense** against knockdown and push effects. When performing movement actions, they may replace one **Simple Action** with one **Free Action**, and they gain advantage on Running-related tests.

birth-favored-recovery-vitality.description: The character naturally recovers from physical harm more efficiently than most, enduring restorative procedures with reduced strain.
birth-favored-recovery-vitality.effet: Vitality (PV) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Vitality that generates Fatigue (typically from non-natural sources), the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1** (1 Fatigue per 3 points recovered instead of the default).

birth-favored-recovery-endurance.description: The character replenishes their physical stamina more efficiently, allowing sustained effort with reduced exhaustion.
birth-favored-recovery-endurance.effet: Endurance (PE) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Endurance that generates Fatigue, the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1**.

birth-favored-recovery-karma.description: The character's fortune and destiny realign more easily, smoothing the strain caused by forced or accelerated recovery.
birth-favored-recovery-karma.effet: Karma (PK) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Karma that generates Fatigue, the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1**.

birth-favored-recovery-spirituality.description: The character's spiritual essence stabilizes quickly after depletion, resisting backlash from restorative effects.
birth-favored-recovery-spirituality.effet: Spirituality (PS) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Spirituality that generates Fatigue, the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1**.

birth-favored-recovery-mana.description: The character channels arcane energies more safely, recovering magical reserves with reduced exhaustion.
birth-favored-recovery-mana.effet: Mana (PM) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Mana that generates Fatigue, the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1**.

birth-favored-recovery-chi.description: The character's internal energy flows more harmoniously, allowing accelerated recovery with less physical or spiritual strain.
birth-favored-recovery-chi.effet: Chi (PC) recovery is increased by **+2**. When the character receives healing to Chi that generates Fatigue, the Fatigue threshold is increased by **+1**.

birth-favored-save-fortune.description: The character is naturally protected by luck or destiny when facing unpredictable outcomes.

birth-favored-save-composure.description: The character maintains emotional control under pressure more reliably than most.

birth-favored-save-insight.description: The character resists deception, illusion, and misdirection with heightened clarity of mind.

birth-favored-save-determination.description: The character's willpower allows them to endure coercion, fear, and mental strain more effectively.

birth-favored-save-prestige.description: The character's social standing and force of presence shield them against reputational or authority-based threats.

birth-favored-save-toughness.description: The character's body withstands physical punishment and harmful effects better than average.

birth-favored-save-reflexes.description: The character reacts instinctively to danger, avoiding sudden threats with superior reflexes.

birth-favored-save-opposition.description: The character excels at actively resisting external forces, particularly supernatural intrusions.
birth-favored-save-opposition.effet: Opposition Saves are increased by **+1**. This bonus also applies to other Saves when they are used specifically to counter magical effects.

birth-examplary.description: Raised as a model individual within their people, the character embodies discipline, cultural standards, and social expectations.

birth-human-ingenuity.description: Humans display remarkable adaptability, either by accumulating raw potential or by excelling naturally in a specific type of situation shaped by their culture.
birth-human-ingenuity.effet: The character gains **+10 PA**, **or** selects one **Aura** (see Auras below).

cult-secundary-specialty.description: The character diversifies their expertise beyond their initial focus, developing a second domain of specialization.
cult-secundary-specialty.effet: The character gains one additional Specialty. This Specialty must meet the same requirements as the first one.

cult-accelerated-learning.description: Humans learn quickly through experience, adapting faster than most to new challenges and disciplines.

cult-auras.description:
An Aura represents a natural human excellence in a specific type of situation.  
When an Aura applies, the character's **natural Prowess** for the relevant test is increased by **+2**, allowing the expenditure of up to **2 PC** to gain up to **+2 adjustments**.  

#### Singularities-Based Auras

| Aura | Applies When the Test… |
|-----|------------------------|
| **Excellence** | Would result in a critical success |
| **Heroicness** | Displays an exploit |
| **Steadiness** | Displays clumsiness or a fumble |
| **Providence** | Displays a strike of luck |
| **Destiny** | Is rerolled through Karma |
| **Stubbornness** | Is attempted again after a previous failure |
| **Altruism** | Benefits others but not the character |
| **Audacity** | Is performed against a figure of authority (in combat, only the leader qualifies) |

#### Save-Based Auras

| Aura | Associated Save |
|-----|------------------|
| **Hardiness** | Robustness |
| **Swiftness** | Reflexes |
| **Unyieldingness** | Determination |
| **Stressless** | Composure |
| **Fatefulness** | Fortune |
| **Insightfulness** | Intuition |
| **Adverseness** | Opposition |
| **Authority** | Prestige (first contact with NPCs only) |

#### Skill Group-Based Auras

| Aura | Applies To |
|-----|------------|
| **Bodycraft** | Athletics / Finesse skill groups |
| **Adverseness** | Combat skill groups (melee attacks only) |
| **Aimfulness** | Combat skill groups (ranged attacks only) |
| **Bulwarkness** | Combat skill groups (defensive actions only) |
| **Stalwartness** | Combat skill groups (tactical actions only) |
| **Greatness** | Social skill groups |
| **Stealthiness** | Stealth skill groups |
| **Deviousness** | Thievery and Subterfuge skill groups |
| **Professionalism** | Craft and Profession skill groups |
| **Wildernessism** | Rural Survival and Hunting skill groups |
| **Urbanism** | Urban Survival and Street skill groups |
| **Persuasiveness** | Social Interaction and Eloquence skill groups |
| **Lorecraft** | Knowledge and Erudition skill groups |
| **Deductiveness** | Investigation skill groups |
| **Pragmatism** | Practical Know-how skill groups |
| **Swiftness** | Sagacity skill groups |
| **Benevolence** | Healing skill groups |
| **Osmoticness** | Arcane skill groups |
| **Tenacity** | Discipline skill groups |
| **Lucidity** | Acuity skill groups |

birth-ancestral.description: The character carries an ancient lineage that instinctively resists supernatural interference with the mind and emotions.
birth-ancestral.effet: The character may reroll tests made to resist magical charms, illusions, and other magical effects that alter the mind or emotions.

birth-far-sight.description: The character perceives the world with exceptional clarity, effortlessly noticing distant details that would escape most individuals.
birth-far-sight.effet: All perception distances and ranged attack distances are increased by **50%**. When precise measurement is unnecessary, the character is simply considered to see farther and more clearly than average.

birth-night-vision.description: The character's eyes are adapted to low-light environments, allowing them to function effectively in darkness.
birth-night-vision.effet: The character can see in dim light and darkness, but not in total darkness. Penalties caused by low light are reduced by **3**, but penalties caused by complete darkness are not reduced.

birth-song-of-the-earth.description: By attuning themselves to a place through quiet communion, the character harmonizes with its subtle rhythms and energies.
birth-song-of-the-earth.effet: The character may spend a short uninterrupted period alone to attune to a specific location (typically limited to a single room). Until attuned to another location, the character gains **+1** to all tests performed there. If the location is natural, this bonus becomes **+2**. During attunement, the character cannot perform any other action.

cult-focus.description: Through deep concentration, the character can momentarily sharpen their perception and execution.
cult-focus.effet: Once per day, the character may reroll one chosen skill test.

birth-soul-connection.description: The character forges profound emotional bonds that transcend distance, manifesting as a constant awareness of those they are linked to.
birth-soul-connection.effet: The character may designate up to **three** individuals as soul-linked. They continuously perceive their general state, emotions, and relative orientation (and roughly their distance). This bond must be based on genuine attachment and cannot be formed casually.

birth-light-feets.description: The character moves with supernatural lightness, barely disturbing the surface they tread upon.
birth-light-feets.effet: The character may walk on water, sinking only once their movement ends. They ignore movement penalties and terrain-based damage, provided they do not end their turn on damaging terrain.

birth-yoke-of-the-waters.description: The character's body requires constant contact with water to function properly, binding their survival to its presence.
birth-yoke-of-the-waters.effet: Each day after the first without significant skin contact with water imposes a **–1 penalty to Constitution**, worsening daily until death if deprivation continues. In exchange, while properly hydrated, the character gains **+1 Fortune**.

birth-impervious.description: The character's body partially absorbs hostile energies, dulling their impact.
birth-impervious.effet: The character gains minor resistance to all energy types, reducing such damage by **25%**.

birth-forgettable-presence.description: When nothing about the character stands out, their presence slips easily from memory.
birth-forgettable-presence.effet: If the character's appearance and behavior are unremarkable, witnesses cannot recall their presence afterward. In combat, the character may halve their menace if they choose to activate this effect.

cult-shadow-walker.description: The character blends instinctively with shadows, moving unseen where others could not.
cult-shadow-walker.effet: The character may attempt to hide in conditions that would normally make hiding impossible, except in completely bare and well-lit spaces. Under favorable conditions, hiding attempts are made with advantage. In combat, menace may be halved if the effect is activated.

birth-soiled-soul.description: The character’s soul has been permanently tainted, bearing scars that cannot be fully cleansed. This corruption does not dominate them outright, but it leaves a lingering imprint that makes them more tolerant—and more vulnerable—to its influence.
birth-soiled-soul.effet: The character’s maximum Corruption threshold is increased by **+2**.

cult-grace.description: The character turns successful evasions into lethal opportunities.
cult-grace.effet: When the character successfully dodges an attack or tactic, they may mark the attacker. Only one target may be marked at a time, and the mark ends at the end of the scene. The character gains a reroll on their next attack or tactical combat action against the marked target.

birth-payback.description: Pain sharpens the character's resolve, fueling precise retaliation.
birth-payback.effet: When the character is hit, they may mark the attacker. Only one target may be marked at a time, and the mark ends at the end of the scene. The character gains a reroll on their next attack or tactical combat action against the marked target.

cult-neverending-hope.description: Even in despair, the character draws strength from loss, turning suffering into resolve.
cult-neverending-hope.effet: Initiative is increased by **+1** for every **5 total points** of Spirituality (PS) and Vitality (PV) lost so far, up to a maximum of **+5**.

birth-dark-surge.description: A dark shard dwells within dark elves, a suffocating echo that dims the natural glow of life in their soul. This shadow is present from birth but remains dormant until adolescence, when it begins to actively consume its host. At that age, dark elven youths are inevitably claimed—by consent or by force—by the secretive organization that governs their people and teaches them how to survive with this inner darkness. When mastered, the shard grants terrifying efficiency; when neglected, it leads swiftly to ruin.
birth-dark-surge.effet:
The character gains an initial **+2 bonus to Initiative**.

After each **Long Rest**, once all normal recoveries have been applied, the character gains **1d8 Corruption**. The character may then attempt a **Determination save** against a Difficulty equal to their current **Shadow Threshold** (0 by default).

The result of this save modifies the Corruption gained:
- **Critical Success:** No Corruption gained  
- **Success:** Corruption gained is reduced by **50%**  
- **Failure:** Full Corruption gained  
- **Critical Failure:** Corruption gained is increased by **50%**

All values are rounded **up**.

After resolving this test, the character’s **Shadow Threshold increases by 1**.

Each time the character participates in a **Confrontation**, their Shadow Threshold also increases by **1**.

If the character dies as a result of Corruption, they do not perish normally. Instead, they transform into a **Creature of Shadow**, an aberration of overwhelming violence and power—an outcome greatly feared by all who know of the Dark Surge.

To mitigate the Dark Surge, the character may consume a dose of **Thaïs Flowers**, reducing their Shadow Threshold by **1d8**. Each dose costs **1 gold piece**.

cult-cantrips.description: The character instinctively channels minor magical effects without formal training.
cult-cantrips.effet: The character may cast level 0 spells as if they had proper access to magic. The magical tradition is chosen among those associated with the character's primary attribute. Maximum Mana (PM) is equal to half the normal maximum. The character may only access one domain or skill. If the character later gains a domain through a proper tradition, maximum PM is recalculated using the higher value, and all spells from that domain may be cast at orison level.

cult-magic-attunement.description: The character resonates deeply with a specific branch of magic.
cult-magic-attunement.effet: The character selects one magic domain. When casting spells from this domain, each die rolled has a minimum value of **3**. When spells from this domain are used against the character, each die rolled has a maximum value of **5**.

cult-creature-tongue.description: The character can sense and convey the immediate intentions of magically attuned beings.
cult-creature-tongue.effet: The character may imperfectly understand or transmit basic intentions to creatures possessing a magic attribute. Complex or abstract concepts cannot be conveyed.

cult-beast-tongue.description: The character communicates instinctively with terrestrial and aerial beasts, including feral creatures.
cult-beast-tongue.effet: As Magic Tongue, but applies to beasts.

cult-fish-tongue.description: The character shares a primal understanding with aquatic creatures and amphibious peoples.
cult-fish-tongue.effet: As Magic Tongue, but applies to aquatic creatures and amphibious ancestries.

cult-spirit-tongue.description: The character perceives the intent of spirits and creatures bound to darkness and the unseen.
cult-spirit-tongue.effet: As Magic Tongue, but applies to spirits, vermin, infernal creatures, and creatures adapted to darkness.

cult-monster-tongue.description: The character understands the raw instincts of monstrous beings.
cult-monster-tongue.effet: As Magic Tongue, but applies to monsters, including semi-monsters.

cult-dragon-tongue.description: The character can perceive the immediate intent of draconic beings.
cult-dragon-tongue.effet: As Magic Tongue, but applies to dragons and drakkans.

birth-tireless-fortitude.description: Dwarves endure combat through sheer resilience, wearing down their enemies through unwavering toughness.
birth-tireless-fortitude.effet: All attack, defense, and tactical rolls made by opponents against the character are reduced by **–1** during combat.

birth-financial-acumen.description: The character possesses a natural sense for value, profit, and material worth.
birth-financial-acumen.effet: The character gains **5% more money** from all monetary gains.

birth-infra-vision.description: The character's eyes are adapted to low-light environments, allowing effective perception underground and in darkness.
birth-infra-vision.effet: The character can see in dim light and darkness, but not in total darkness. Penalties caused by low light are reduced by **3**, while penalties caused by complete darkness are not reduced.

birth-expansive-memory.description: The character's memory is exceptionally reliable, capable of retaining vast amounts of technical, historical, or practical information.
birth-expansive-memory.effet: The character's Memory score is increased by **+5**.

cult-forethought.description: The character is habitually prepared, rarely caught without the tools they need.
cult-forethought.effet: As long as the character owns the appropriate tools or items and has sufficient inventory space, they are assumed to have them available when needed. No Intuition test is required to determine whether the character remembered to bring necessary equipment.

cult-efficient-repairs.description: Dwarven craftsmanship prioritizes durability and efficiency, especially when restoring damaged equipment.
cult-efficient-repairs.effet: The cost **or** the duration of repair actions is halved (player chooses which applies each time).

cult-mercantile-culture.description: Trade and wealth are deeply ingrained in the character's cultural background.
cult-mercantile-culture.effet: The character gains **5% more money** from all monetary gains. This bonus stacks with **Financial Acumen**.

birth-hardness.description: The character's body is physically dense and resistant to harm.
birth-hardness.effet: Physical Protection is increased by **+1**, and the character can sustain one additional scratch wound.

cult-bloodline-pride.description: The character draws strength and confidence from their lineage, invoking ancestral achievements when it truly matters.
cult-bloodline-pride.effet: Once per day, if the character meaningfully references their lineage or family accomplishments during a skill test, all dice rolled for that test have a minimum value of **4** (this effect cannot generate singularities). In combat, this may be used, but requires a speech action appropriate to the length of the explanation.

cult-unyielding.description: Refusing to fall, the character clings to consciousness through sheer stubborn will.
cult-unyielding.effet: Once per scene, when the character would lose their last Vitality (PV) or Spirituality (PS) point, that resource is set to **0** instead of going into negative values.

cult-stubbornness.description: Experience hardens the character against afflictions they have already endured.
cult-stubbornness.effet: The character may reroll a test to resist venom, poison, sickness, curses, or rare conditions that they have already experienced at least once. For common conditions, this applies if the character has experienced the condition earlier in the same scene.

birth-natural-wa.description: The character is born with innate weapons and natural protection, fully integrated into their body and fighting style.
birth-natural-wa.effet: The character possesses **Category 1 natural weapons** and **Category 1 natural armor**, and is trained in the use of both.

birth-stronger-natural-weapons.description: The character's natural weapons are more developed and deadly than average.
birth-stronger-natural-weapons.effet: The character's natural weapons are upgraded to **Category 2**. The character is trained in their use.

birth-stronger-natural-armor.description: The character's hide, scales, or shell offer enhanced protection.
birth-stronger-natural-armor.effet: The character's natural armor is upgraded to **Category 2**. The character is trained in its use.

birth-unyielding-bulk.description: The character's massive build allows them to carry far more than most.
birth-unyielding-bulk.effet: Bulk capacity is increased by **+2**, and total bulk capacity is increased by **+4**.

birth-teamworker.description: The character thrives when acting alongside others.
birth-teamworker.effet: Once per scene, the character may reroll a test when helping an ally. Allies may also reroll when helping the character.

birth-good-company.description: The character's presence uplifts those around them.
birth-good-company.effet: Once per day, after spending at least a short period of time with someone, the character may grant that person **temporary Karma Points** lasting until the end of a full cycle. This cannot be used on self and does not stack. Allies resting near the character gain **+1 Recovery**, which also applies to the character if at least one ally benefits.

cult-an-ally-in-each-city.description: The character's community extends far and wide.
cult-an-ally-in-each-city.effet: In any city of at least 1,000 inhabitants, the character knows at least one ally from their community. This ally can provide limited, non-burdensome assistance at the GM's discretion.

birth-hunters-edge.description: The character strikes with lethal efficiency when hunting or using natural weapons.
birth-hunters-edge.effet: The character may reroll tests made with natural weapons, or when hunting a proper target using hunting weapons.

cult-blood-lust.description: Wounding foes fuels the character's endurance.
cult-blood-lust.effet: When the character deals Vitality damage with a melee or close-combat attack, they recover **1 Endurance (PE)** for every **3 Vitality points** inflicted (subject to Fatigue).

birth-blood-critical.description: The character's critical strikes exploit existing wounds with savage precision.
birth-blood-critical.effet: The character's critical combat actions gain a bonus to the roll equal to the target's wound level.

birth-survivability.description: The character remains harder to bring down as long as they are not stunned or shocked.
birth-survivability.effet: While not shocked, passive defenses are increased by **+1** when armored, or **+2** when unarmored (natural armor does not count as armor).

birth-hunter.description: The character pierces defenses with ruthless efficiency.
birth-hunter.effet: The character ignores **one category of natural armor**. All weapons gain **+1 minimum die value**, and natural weapons gain an additional **+1 minimum die value**.

birth-royal-roar.description: The character's roar galvanizes action and intent.
birth-royal-roar.effet: Once per scene, the character may reroll a Shout action.

cult-king-shoot.description: The character inspires allies through commanding presence.
cult-king-shoot.effet: Once per turn, the character may use a non-standard Shout simple action to grant all nearby allies **d8 + mCHA Morale Points** for the scene. This may be combined with a standard Shout action by spending a Free Action.

cult-tyran-shoot.description: The character crushes enemy resolve through sheer dominance.
cult-tyran-shoot.effet: Same as King's Shout, but instead removes the same amount of Morale Points from all nearby enemies. No defense or save applies. May be combined with a standard Shout action by spending a Free Action.

cult-guardian-shoot.description: The character shields allies through commanding warnings.
cult-guardian-shoot.effet: Once per turn, the character may use a non-standard Shout simple action to grant all nearby allies a mark that gives advantage on their next defense action. The mark lasts until the character's next turn and affects the character as well. May be combined with a standard Shout action by spending a Free Action.

cult-frenzy-shout.description: The character spreads chaos and reckless aggression.
cult-frenzy-shout.effet: Once per turn, the character may attempt a non-standard Shout simple action (Difficulty **15**) to mark all nearby enemies, giving them disadvantage on their next defense action. The mark lasts until the character's next turn. May be combined with a standard Shout action by spending a Free Action.

birth-speedster.description: The character is accustomed to constant motion and rapid repositioning.
birth-speedster.effet: The character may reroll speed-based movement tests and treats movement actions as familiar and routine.

birth-fast-to-act.description: The character reacts instantly when conflict erupts.
birth-fast-to-act.effet: The character gains **+10 Speed** during the first round of a confrontation.

birth-maniac.description: The character pushes beyond normal limits in moments of frenzy.
birth-maniac.effet: The character may act during a third round, suffering **–20 to all actions** and disadvantage on all tests. They may also borrow up to **10 Initiative** from their next turn.

birth-stability.effet: The character may reroll balance-based tests. Initiative rolls have a minimum die value of **3** (cannot trigger singularities).

birth-bull-stance.description: The character plants themselves like an immovable force.
birth-bull-stance.effet: Defense against knockdown and push effects is increased by **+5**.

birth-danger-sense.description: The character instinctively feels nearby threats.
birth-danger-sense.effet: The character senses the presence of danger in the area. Hostile intentions are perceived only when an act is imminent or premeditated. If danger is nearby, the character can identify its source.

birth-fear-sense.description: The character senses fear as a tangible presence.
birth-fear-sense.effet: The character perceives fear in the surrounding area and can identify its origin if it is nearby.

cult-hornamental-magic-tradition.description: The character channels magic through raw physical resilience.
cult-hornamental-magic-tradition.effet: The character may choose **CON** as a free magic tradition attribute. Accessible domains are limited to **Metal, War, and Law**, with rare exceptions granted by story and GM discretion (up to MD).

cult-affinity-with-totem.description: The character resonates naturally with their cultural totem.
cult-affinity-with-totem.effet: The character gains an Affinity linked to one attribute associated with their Totem. By default, a Totem provides two attributes.

cult-align-with-totem.description:
Through ritual alignment, the character invokes their Totem's supernatural power.  
**Cost:** 1 PP
cult-align-with-totem.effet:
Once per day, the character may use a Totem power a number of times equal to the Degrees of Success obtained on a Religion test performed the previous day (Difficulty 10; at least 1 Degree at 15).

- **FOR / CHA – Domination:** The target suffers disadvantage on a test involving the character.  
- **CON / VOL – Resilience:** All disadvantages on a test are negated.  
- **SAG / PER – Focus:** The target loses all advantages on a test involving the character.  
- **AGI / RUS – Guile:** The target's advantages become disadvantages on a test involving the character.  
- **DEX / INT – Coordination:** The target loses one advantage, which the character gains for all resulting actions.

If the Totem grants the same power twice, it also provides:  
- **Balance:** The character gains advantage on a test.

cult-erudition.description: The character's accumulated knowledge can be recalled with precision.
cult-erudition.effet: Once per day, the character may reroll an Erudition or Knowledge skill test.

cult-bookworm.description: The character can decipher almost any written language.
cult-bookworm.effet: The character can read all living or dead languages at a rudimentary level, understanding the subject but not the nuance. They gain a high bonus on reading attempts.

birth-plagued.description: The character's attacks carry a lingering corruption.
birth-plagued.effet: When hit by the character's natural weapons, targets gain the Poison condition with a number of charges equal to the action's roll score. The target may attempt a Toughness test to reduce the charges (Difficulty based on **mCON** instead of **mDEX**). On a singularity, this may also apply to non-natural weapons.

cult-malicious-expertise.description: The character excels at inflicting lasting harm and debilitation.
cult-malicious-expertise.effet: The character's Expertise is increased by **+1** when applying conditions.

birth-quick-feets.description: The character moves with exceptional agility and lightness.

cult-lightfoot.description: The character barely disturbs the ground beneath them.
cult-lightfoot.effet: Falling damage is halved. The character ignores walking penalties and terrain-based damage, provided they do not end their turn on damaging terrain.

birth-reach.description: The character's movements and strikes extend farther than expected.
birth-reach.effet: Reach is increased by **+1** for all weapons.

birth-speed.description: The character's raw speed surpasses normal limits.

birth-heart-stead.description: The character flows effortlessly through motion.
birth-heart-stead.effet: The character gains one free **Move** action per scene and one free **Step** action per turn.

cult-flutterstep.description: The character dances through danger with instinctive grace.
cult-flutterstep.effet: The character gains one free **Dodge** action per scene, and an additional free Dodge action usable only while flying.

birth-all-senses-out.description: The character remains instinctively aware of their surroundings at all times.
birth-all-senses-out.effet: The character may always attempt an Acuity test regardless of circumstances (including sleep). They automatically gain access to an appropriate perception test without requiring an Intuition test to notice that something is wrong.

birth-all-eyes-on-me.description: The character's presence is impossible to ignore.
birth-all-eyes-on-me.effet: The character cannot be forgotten. In confrontations, they may attempt one free emotion-based action per target (area-of-effect actions count each target).

cult-karmic-vision.description: The character perceives the hidden currents of fate and morality.
cult-karmic-vision.effet: The character may learn and use the **Sixth Sense**, allowing them to perceive a target's alignment, persona (temperament and character), and current resources.

cult-supra-vision.description: The character perceives how others perceive.
cult-supra-vision.effet: The character may learn and use the **Seventh Sense**, allowing them to know whether they (or another target) are being seen, heard, or sensed.

birth-wood-skin.description: The character's skin is composed of bark and living wood.
birth-wood-skin.effet: The character gains **1 Resistance**, **3 Absorption**, and **1 Protection** at all times, but is vulnerable to fire.

birth-natural-armor.description: The character's body provides inherent protection.

birth-immaculate.description: The character recovers with remarkable purity and efficiency.
birth-immaculate.effet: Recovery is increased by **+1**. Any Fatigue gained through healing is reduced by **1**.

birth-natural-regeneration.effet: The character recovers **2 Endurance (PE)** per round while in a scene. This recovery may generate Fatigue normally. Outside of scenes, Endurance returns to maximum unless the character chooses to prevent it to avoid Fatigue.

birth-full-recovery.description: The character's overall recovery capacity is exceptional.

birth-restless.description: The character endures strain longer than most.

birth-keep-best.description: Luck favors the character even when fate is rewritten.
birth-keep-best.effet: Once per rerolling dice through Karma or reroll effects, the character keeps the best result between the original and the new re-roll dice.

birth-above-average.description: The character's failures are rarely complete.
birth-above-average.effet: Any die showing a **1** is treated as a **2** instead. This effect may trigger singularities normally and may be ignored voluntarily.

cult-second-chance-gambit.description: The character dares one final attempt when all others would stop.
cult-second-chance-gambit.effet: Once per day, the character may retry a failed action that normally forbids retries. A second failure results in aggravated consequences or additional Fatigue. The character gains a moderate bonus to the relevant skill for this attempt.

cult-initial-flow.description: The character enters scenes already in motion.
cult-initial-flow.effet: The character starts every scene with **1 temporary Chi (PC)**, increased to **2** in confrontation scenes.

birth-cautious.description: The character avoids disastrous mistakes through careful restraint.
birth-cautious.effet: The critical failure threshold is increased by **5** (a failure of 15+ is required to trigger a critical failure).

birth-favored-mind.description: The character's mind is deeply attuned to natural patterns.
birth-favored-mind.effet: All mental attributes gain **+2**, while **Strength** and **Constitution** suffer a **–4 penalty**.

birth-motionless.description: The character remains unmoved by emotional manipulation.
birth-motionless.effet: Defenses against emotion-based actions are increased by **+5**, and such actions cannot critically succeed against the character.

birth-too-pretty-to-hit.description: The character's allure disrupts hostile intent.
birth-too-pretty-to-hit.effet: Defenses are increased by **+2** against enemies who may be attracted to the character, or **+3** if the target is actively seduced for the scene. Defenses are reduced by **–1** against others. Applies to both physical and mental situations.

birth-guiled-movement.description: The character instinctively compensates for their weaknesses.
birth-guiled-movement.effet: The character's lowest defenses are increased by **+2**.

birth-vigilance.effet: Defenses against counterattacks are increased by **+2**.

cult-totem-carving.description: The character carves symbolic totems infused with fortune.
cult-totem-carving.effet: By spending a small activity, the character may carve a totem linked to a specific activity. The bearer may shatter it to reroll a skill test. Totems have no monetary value. Crafting Difficulty **15**; on a critical success, the totem may be used twice.

cult-charm-carving.description: The character creates charms bound to individuals.
cult-charm-carving.effet: As Totem Carving, but applies to a specific person and allows rerolling a Save instead of a skill test.

cult-tattoo.description: The character bears a meaningful tattoo tied to mastery.
cult-tattoo.effet: The clumsiness range for tests using a specific skill is reduced by **1**.

cult-improved-tattoo.description:
The tattoo's power is refined and intensified.  
**Cost:** 1 PP
cult-improved-tattoo.effet: The exploit range of the tattoo's skill is increased by **+1**, stackable up to a maximum final range of **6**.

cult-training.description: The character receives structured martial instruction.
cult-training.effet: The character gains one level of training in melee weapons, ranged weapons, or armor.

cult-improved-training.description: The character broadens their martial expertise.
cult-improved-training.effet: The character gains **two levels of training** across two different choices among melee weapons, ranged weapons, or armor.

birth-celestial-light.description: Sunlight sustains the character's celestial nature.
birth-celestial-light.effet: After a few minutes of sunlight exposure, the character's needs for sleep, food, and water are halved. Daily recovery requires only half the normal rest duration.

birth-celestial-hearth.description: A divine core shields the character from corruption and despair.
birth-celestial-hearth.effet: Corruption maximum is increased by **+2**. Saves against sickness, venom, poison, and toxins gain advantage. Self Morale is increased by **+2**.

birth-eternal-youth.description: Time barely touches the character's body.
birth-eternal-youth.effet: Physical aging progresses at one year per ten years after reaching maturity, maintaining peak condition indefinitely.

birth-flight-movement.description: The character's aerial form is both swift and protected.
birth-flight-movement.effet: Allure is increased by **+2** while flying, and passive defense against all magic is increased by **+2**.

birth-windborne.effet: The character gains perfect flight maneuverability and **+1 passive defense** when flying high above the ground.

birth-waterborne.effet: The character gains perfect swimming maneuverability and **+1 passive defense** while fully submerged.

cult-whimsical-magic-tradition.description: The character channels magic through chance and unpredictability.
cult-whimsical-magic-tradition.effet: The character may choose **LUK** as a free tradition attribute. Accessible domains are limited to **Illusion, Charm, and Chaos**, with rare narrative exceptions.

cult-celestial-powers.description:
Celestials wield ideal-driven magic rooted in abstract virtues.  
**Cost:** 1 PP
cult-celestial-powers.effet: The character selects one **Edict** (ideal). Spells aligned with that Edict gain advantage and are reinforced. Edicts are morally neutral and represent rigid cosmic principles such as Justice, Balance, or Order.

birth-chromatic-blood.description: The character's blood carries latent chromatic energy.
birth-chromatic-blood.effet: Each day, the character gains up to **5 + mEQU Chroma Points**, which may be spent to prevent Endurance loss.

birth-natural-resistance.description: The character resists energies tied to their chromatic nature.
birth-natural-resistance.effet: The character gains light resistance to all domains and energies linked to their kind or color.

birth-surnatural-resistance.description: The character's resistances deepen beyond the mundane.
birth-surnatural-resistance.effet: All natural resistances are upgraded from light to medium.

cult-dragon-breath.description: The character unleashes raw elemental force.
cult-dragon-breath.effet: As Dragon Powers, but limited to a cone-shaped effect using an energy tied to the chosen domain.

cult-dragon-powers.description:
**Cost:** 1 PP  
Drakkans carry within their veins a diluted echo of true draconic power. Where true dragons wield Chromatic Power directly through their dragon heart—an overwhelming, primordial force—Drakkans can only access a minute fragment of that power through their blood. This fragment allows them to invoke crude yet potent magical phenomena tied to a specific chromatic domain. Though vastly inferior to true dragons in mastery and magnitude, this inherited magic remains instinctive, raw, and difficult to counter through conventional arcane means.
cult-dragon-powers.effet:
Chroma Points granted by **Chromatic Blood** may be spent to invoke **Dragonic Powers**. These powers represent **Chromatic Magic**, a fast but simple form of magic distinct from spellcasting.

- As a **Simple Action**, once per turn, the character may produce a spell-like phenomenon based on a **simple spell** from the chromatic domain associated with their blood.  
- Most Drakkan types are tied to a **predefined set of domains**. This choice is made at character creation and cannot be changed later.  
- **Balance (EQU)** is used to perform the test associated with Dragonic Powers.  
- A dedicated skill group and skill may be learned to improve the odds of successfully invoking these powers.  

Dragonic Powers may be used at **spell level 0**, increased by **+1 spell level for every 4 character ranks**.

Magical tools do not function properly with Dragonic Powers. However, the character may still add their **rank** to the power's roll as if using an appropriate magical tool, regardless of the power's level.

The **Chroma Point cost** of a Dragonic Power is equal to the **minimum mana cost of a comparable spell** and **cannot be reduced by any means**. The **action difficulty** is also based on that same comparable spell.

Dragonic Powers are **not spells**:
- They cannot be countered.  
- They are not treated as regular spells.  
- They are unaffected by arcane actions or effects that specifically interact with spells.

Depending on the Drakkan type, Chroma Points may alternatively be spent as if they were another resource:
- **Red:** Rage  
- **Blue:** Chi  
- **Green:** Guard  
- **Yellow:** Adrenaline  
- **Purple:** Mana  
- **Black:** Vitality  
- **White:** Karma  
- **Grey:** Spirituality

birth-high-natural-regeneration.description: The character's body regenerates at an extreme rate, far beyond normal biological limits.
birth-high-natural-regeneration.effet: This functions as **Natural Regeneration**, but all recovered values are **doubled**.

birth-reserve-stock.description: The character's body instinctively stores surplus energy for later use.
birth-reserve-stock.effet: When resting, excess recovery may be stored at a **1:3 ratio**. Stored resources may reach up to **10% of the character's current maximum**.

birth-last-stand.description: The character refuses to fall even when pushed beyond mortal limits.
birth-last-stand.effet: While Vitality (PV) is negative, the character gains **medium resistance to all physical damage**. While Spirituality (PS) is negative, the character gains **medium resistance to all mental damage**. The character may also ignore up to the **first two levels of wounds and traumas**.

cult-fatigueless.description: The character shrugs off strain more easily than others.
cult-fatigueless.effet: Stress gained is always reduced by **1**.

birth-oversized-tool-bearer.description: The character can wield equipment far larger than what their size would normally allow.
birth-oversized-tool-bearer.effet: The character may use **Category 7 items** (tools, armor, and weapons), which are typically custom-made.

birth-steady-hands.description: The character maintains firm control even over unwieldy equipment.
birth-steady-hands.effet: The required grip for weapons is reduced by **3**.

cult-resourceful.description: The character always finds a way to make do with what is available.
cult-resourceful.effet: The character can always find or improvise a usable weapon or tool in their surroundings and is considered trained in all improvised weapons and tools. Common sense and context determine applicability.

cult-not-picky.description: The character is accustomed to using subpar equipment.
cult-not-picky.effet: Penalties caused by item degradation or poor-quality equipment are reduced by **1**.

cult-scavenger.description: The character excels at reclaiming value from the aftermath of conflict.
cult-scavenger.effet: When recycling, harvesting, or looting money from defeated enemies, the amount gained is increased by **10%**. Context and logic apply.

cult-debt-of-pain.description: The character temporarily defers suffering, pushing pain aside through sheer will.
cult-debt-of-pain.effet: Once per scene, when receiving damage, the character may use a reaction to ignore that damage for the duration of the scene. The ignored damage (before penetration or mitigation) is recorded. At the end of the scene, the character suffers the recorded damage as **piercing damage**, ignoring armor and protection. If an Endurance test is successful, this use does not count against the once-per-scene limit. Applies to both physical and mental damage.

cult-strongness.description: The character's physiology and mindset blunt harmful effects while amplifying beneficial ones.
cult-strongness.effet: Negative conditions are applied with **2 fewer charges**, while positive conditions are applied with **2 additional charges**.

birth-scapegoats.description: The character instinctively draws blame and hostility onto themselves.
birth-scapegoats.effet: The character may attempt to take responsibility for a situation, redirecting blame toward themselves. Affected targets may resist with an **Insight save** against the character's **mental expertise**. In confrontations, this requires a **Simple Action**. Enemies who ignore this effect and continue targeting their original objective suffer disadvantage on their tests. This functions as a provocation without requiring a direct opposed roll.

birth-coward-insights.description: The character survives by redirecting danger rather than confronting it directly.
birth-coward-insights.effet: Once per scene, when targeted by an attack, the character may redirect it to an adjacent willing ally. The new target may defend normally.

cult-courageous-presence.description: The character's resolve inspires others in tense situations.
cult-courageous-presence.effet: During confrontations, the character grants **+1 Morale**.

cult-miserable-presence.description: The character's suffering is contagious, but fuels their own determination.
cult-miserable-presence.effet: During confrontations, the character grants **–1 Morale** to others, but gains **+2 Morale** themselves.

cult-terrible-presence.description: The character radiates oppression and despair.
cult-terrible-presence.effet: During confrontations, the character grants **–1 Morale** and additionally reduces enemy Morale by **2**.

cult-wild-urges.description: The character's primal drives surge far beyond restraint.
cult-wild-urges.effet: Maximum **Rage**, **Guard**, or **Adrenaline** is increased by **+4**. At the start of a scene, the character may begin with **4 + mCON** points in one of these resources.

cult-animosity.description: The character harbors a deep and enduring hatred toward a specific category of creatures, often rooted in ancestral conflicts, cultural trauma, or personal history. This animosity sharpens both their aggression and their vigilance when dealing with such foes.
cult-animosity.effet:
The character gains a **+1 bonus to all battle tests** aimed at **attacking**, **defending**, or **applying tactical moves against** targets belonging to the chosen creature type.  

When selecting this particularity, choose one of the following Animosity types:

- **Human Animosity** — Targets the human race.  
- **Elf Animosity** — Targets the elven race.  
- **Dwarf Animosity** — Targets the dwarven race.  
- **Feral Animosity** — Targets feral peoples and feral creatures.  
- **Vermin Animosity** — Targets vermin races, including their monstrous variants.  
- **Magic Animosity** — Targets creatures considered magical, typically those possessing a magic-related attribute.  
- **Spawn Animosity** — Targets spawn-like creatures: servile or identity-less beings such as underdark engence and, more broadly, undead.  
- **Monster Animosity** — Targets monsters of all kinds.  
- **Beast Animosity** — Targets creatures of the animal kingdom, including feral beasts.

Animosity applies whenever the target clearly belongs to the selected category, as determined by the GM.

cult-pack-instinct.description: The character instinctively perceives those they consider part of their pack, sensing presence, distress, and cohesion without conscious effort.

cult-feline-instincts.description: The character intuitively perceives subtle routes, hidden angles, and indirect paths that allow bypassing obstacles through agility, positioning, or verticality rather than brute force.
cult-feline-instincts.effet: Implicit paths, bypasses, climbs, and agile routes around obstacles.

cult-king-instincts.description: The character instinctively senses figures of authority, dominance, or influence, perceiving not only their presence but also the nuances of their power and legitimacy.
cult-king-instincts.effet: Leaders, powerful individuals, authority figures, and influence structures.

cult-survival-instincts.description: The character's instincts constantly scan their surroundings for anything that could preserve life or avert imminent death.
cult-survival-instincts.effet: Survival-relevant elements such as food, water, shelter, tools, and immediate solutions to mortal danger.

cult-hunting-instinct.description: The character perceives prey through signs, patterns, and weakness, entering a focused state when pursuing a target as part of a deliberate hunt.
cult-hunting-instinct.effet: Prey being hunted, including non-bestial targets when framed as a ritualized hunt.

cult-freedom-instinct.description: The character instinctively senses opportunities to escape, break free, or avoid confinement, without relying on physical bypasses like climbing or squeezing.
cult-freedom-instinct.effet: Means of escape, liberation, or avoidance of capture and restraint.

cult-alert-instinct.description: The character instinctively detects threats that are concealed or about to strike.
cult-alert-instinct.effet: Ambushes, traps, and hidden hostile beings.

cult-bestial-instincts.description: The character perceives primal behaviors, dominance cues, and instinct-driven intentions in creatures acting on raw impulse.

cult-oath-instinct.description: The character instinctively senses the presence and weight of spoken bonds, promises, and vows.
cult-oath-instinct.effet: Oaths, promises, pacts, and the relationships created by binding words.

cult-venal-instinct.description: The character instinctively senses the presence of wealth and objects of material value.
cult-venal-instinct.effet: Lootable wealth such as gold, gems, precious items, and valuables.

cult-watery-instinct.description: The character instinctively perceives water in all its manifestations and movements.
cult-watery-instinct.effet: Water sources, currents, humidity, and bodies of water in any form.

cult-unknown-instinct.description: The character feels the presence of things that are unexplored, hidden, or not yet understood.

cult-blood-instincts.description: The character instinctively senses spilled blood and its lingering traces.
cult-blood-instincts.effet: Shed blood, blood trails, and evidence of injury or violence.

cult-curious-instinct.description: The character instinctively notices what is out of place, unusual, or worthy of investigation.

cult-practical-instinct.description: The character instinctively focuses on useful information and actionable clues.
cult-practical-instinct.effet: Practical information, evidence, and meaningful clues.

cult-furious-instincts.description: The character senses rising violence and emotional volatility, as well as places marked by recent or habitual bloodshed.
cult-furious-instincts.effet: Imminent violence, uncontrolled anger, emotional outbursts, recent fights, and lingering violent tension.

cult-malicious-instinct.description: The character instinctively detects cold or calculated intent to cause harm.
cult-malicious-instinct.effet: Malicious intent, betrayal, manipulation, corruption, and moral decay.

cult-vermin-instinct.description: The character instinctively perceives the presence and dynamics of the weak, submissive, or easily manipulated.
cult-vermin-instinct.effet: Minions, underlings, powerless individuals, and highly influenceable people.

cult-avid-instinct.description: The character's senses latch onto anything that stirs their desire—treasure, opportunity, or the faint trace of something worth having. Where others pass by, they notice.

cult-affinity.description:
An Affinity represents a situational harmony between the character and a specific environment, context, or mode of action. When conditions are met, the character performs more efficiently at critical moments.

**Common Effect (all Affinities):**
When a test performed under the conditions of an Affinity results in a **Singularity**, the character gains a **+1 bonus** to that test.

- Multiple Affinities may apply to the same test and can stack.
- Affinities only apply when the fictional conditions are clearly met, as determined by the GM.

#### Environmental Affinities

- **Forest Affinity** — Lush, forested, or overgrown environments; urban gardens also qualify.  
- **Plains Affinity** — Open plains or wide empty spaces; large areas devoid of notable scenery also qualify.  
- **Desertic Affinity** — Arid or barren environments; completely empty spaces also qualify.  
- **Cold Affinity** — Cold environments, natural or artificial.  
- **Heat Affinity** — Hot environments, natural or artificial.  
- **Mountain Affinity** — Mountainous, rocky, or cliffside environments.  
- **Subterranean Affinity** — Underground environments such as caves, tunnels, and caverns.  
- **Filth Affinity** — Extremely dirty or polluted environments (sewers, swamps, refuse-filled areas).  
- **Water Affinity** — Flooded or humid environments; rain, shores, or full immersion always qualify.  
- **Height Affinity** — Elevated environments or situations involving vertical dominance.  
- **Big Affinity** — Vast or disproportionate spaces; applies only against **large targets (Size 15+)**.  
- **Small Affinity** — Tight or cramped environments; applies only against **small targets (Size 5 or lower)**.  
- **Darkness Affinity** — Very dark environments defined by strong shadow contrast, even during daytime.  
- **Light Affinity** — Extremely bright environments with strong light contrast, including direct sunlight.  
- **Calm Affinity** — Silent and calm environments; any noise, including the character's own, breaks the condition.  
- **Road Affinity** — Travel situations, journeys, nomadic movement, or being “on the road.”  
- **Dream Affinity** — Dreamscapes and oneiric environments.  
- **Field Affinity** — Large-scale battlefields involving multiple groups and widespread conflict.  
- **Origin-Specific Environment Affinity** — An environment strongly tied to the character's origin or culture.

#### Situational & Behavioral Affinities

- **Pack Affinity** — Presence of nearby allies.  
- **Barehands Affinity** — Actions performed unarmed or using natural weapons.  
- **Flanking Affinity** — Actions performed against a flanked opponent.  
- **Prudence Affinity** — Actions that result in clumsiness or similar negative singularities.  
- **Chasing Affinity** — Pursuits, chases, and sustained running actions.  
- **Rivalry Affinity** — Actions that clearly reinforce an established rivalry.  
- **Honor and Oath Affinity** — Actions taken to uphold vows, promises, rules, or personal honor.  
- **Blood Affinity** — Actions involving blood: sensing, tracking, identifying, or exploiting it.  
- **Curiosity Affinity** — Actions driven by curiosity or investigation.  
- **Ingenious Affinity** — Actions based on clever planning, ruses, or innovative ideas.  
- **City Affinity** — Actions tied to the notable structure or nature of an urban environment.  
- **Distance Affinity** — Actions involving significant distance; ranged attacks only apply at long range or beyond.  
- **Ambidextry Affinity** — Actions performed using the non-dominant hand.  
- **Immaterial Affinity** — Actions involving magic or intangible forces.  
- **Perfectionist Affinity** — Actions using skills of rank 3 or higher and contributing to improvement or refinement.  
- **Motion Affinity** — Actions tied to emotions or emotional expression.  
- **Battle Affinity** — Actions related to warfare or combat context, excluding direct combat actions.  
- **Art Affinity** — Actions related to artistic creation or expression.  
- **Vengeance Affinity** — Actions taken to fulfill revenge.  
- **Luck Affinity** — Actions involving Karma usage.  
- **Order Affinity** — Actions that uphold rules, commands, or obligations.  
- **Chaos Affinity** — Actions opposing authority, disobeying orders, or rejecting structure.  
- **Selfishness Affinity** — Actions that benefit only the character, even in the long term.  
- **Community Affinity** — Actions that benefit others but not the character.

#### Combat Affinities

- **Unarmored Affinity** — Combat actions performed while wearing no armor (natural armor does not count).  
- **Wounded Affinity** — Combat actions targeting a wounded opponent (beyond superficial scratches).  
- **Pristine Affinity** — Combat actions while all primary resources are at maximum, including Endurance.  
- **Slow Affinity** — Combat actions against a single target performed after that target's turn.  
- **Quick Affinity** — Combat actions against a single target performed before that target's turn.  
- **Resistance Affinity** — Defensive actions.

#### Material & Craft Affinities

- **Metal Affinity** — Actions involving metal work, identification, or observation.  
- **Stone Affinity** — Actions involving stone or rock.  
- **Leather Affinity** — Actions involving leather or hides.  
- **Fabric Affinity** — Actions involving cloth or textiles.  
- **Mineral Affinity** — Actions involving minerals or gemstones.  
- **Poison Affinity** — Actions involving poisons, venoms, or toxic substances.  
- **Wood Affinity** — Actions involving wood.

#### Knowledge & Technique Affinities

- **Knowledge Affinity** — Actions involving knowledge or learning.  
- **Code Affinity** — Actions aligned with the character's personal code of honor.  
- **Altitude Affinity** — Actions performed from a height relative to the target or objective.  
- **Repair Affinity** — Actions aimed at repairing objects or structures.  
- **Arcane Affinity** — Actions involving arcane principles.  
- **Science Affinity** — Actions involving scientific methods or reasoning.  
- **Technology Affinity** — Actions involving technological devices or systems.  
- **Magic Affinity** — Actions involving magical effects or spellcasting.

#### Risk & Conflict Affinities

- **Thievery Affinity** — Actions involving illegal or illicit activities.  
- **Food Affinity** — Actions involving food preparation, acquisition, or consumption.  
- **Sacrifice Affinity** — Actions that benefit others while causing loss or hardship to the character.  
- **Oratory Affinity** — Actions performed before an audience capable of judging the outcome.  
- **Custom Affinity** — Actions related to customs, beliefs, etiquette, or traditions.  
- **Adaptative Affinity** — Actions taken after changing approach following failure or obstruction.  
- **Peril Affinity** — Actions taken while facing potentially lethal danger.  
- **Strive Affinity** — Actions attempted in a weakened or tactically delicate situation.  
- **Adversity Affinity** — Actions with very low objective chances of success; applies when Difficulty is **20+**.  
- **Escape Affinity** — Actions aimed at fleeing or avoiding danger.  
- **Treachery Affinity** — Actions involving deception, cheating, or betrayal.

birth-black-cat.description: Something about this character seems to invert the natural order of misfortune. Those who move against them find that fate itself bristles at the presumption.
birth-black-cat.effet: Karma expenditures aimed at altering an action that is directly detrimental to the character and targets them directly or indirectly are doubled.

birth-white-cat.description: Fortune has a way of leaning toward this character. What benefits them seems to flow more easily, as though the world has quietly decided to be generous.
birth-white-cat.effet: Karma expenditures aimed at altering an action that directly benefits the character, whether by targeting them directly or indirectly, are divided by 2, rounded up.

birth-wild-sentinel.description: This character never fully lowers their guard. Even at rest, something in them remains watchful — a deep, instinctive readiness that predates conscious thought.
birth-wild-sentinel.effet: The character and their close allies do not need to prepare the first defense of a turn; if they do, they gain an advantage during that first defense.

cult-4th-breakfast.description: For this character, food is not merely sustenance — it is ritual, comfort, and the quiet engine of their remarkable resilience. A second helping is never wasted; a third is practically medicinal.
cult-4th-breakfast.effet: Each meal beyond the two normal daily meals grants 2 temporary PK that lasts until the end of the day. Meals must be spaced at least 2 hours apart and require/cost the same as a normal meal.

cult-demanding.description: This character holds themselves — and those around them — to a standard that leaves little room for the acceptable. Precision is expected. Excellence is the floor, not the ceiling.

cult-excellency.description: When this character acts at their best, they surpass themselves. Moments of brilliance do not merely succeed — they resonate, as though the action itself demanded to be done perfectly.
cult-excellency.effet: On a critical hit, the character receives a bonus of 2 to their roll.

birth-sturdy-back.description: A life spent in motion — carrying their own body across vast ground — has built this character's frame into something exceptional. Their back does not simply endure: it bears.
birth-sturdy-back.effet: The character's maximum total bulk is increased by 6.

cult-momentum-breaker.description: Once this character commits to disrupting an opponent, the sheer weight of their presence makes it nearly impossible to ignore. They do not merely interrupt — they derail.
cult-momentum-breaker.effet: An opponent making a saving throw to avoid being interrupted by the character does so at a disadvantage.

cult-force-of-nature.description: The character's body is deeply attuned to natural patterns.
cult-force-of-nature.effet: Gets **Strength** and **Constitution** improvement by **4**, but all mind attributes suffers from a **-2 penalty**.
